claims.statement: The reviewed tool is the canonical minio/mc project, not a fork.
claims.qualification: minio/mc is the canonical AGPLv3 MinIO project; no fork was involved.

claims.statement: mc is written in Go.
claims.qualification: go.mod declares go 1.23.0; the smoke binary self-reported go1.24.6.

claims.statement: mc is licensed under the GNU Affero General Public License v3.
claims.qualification: The source license header and the GitHub license metadata agree on AGPL-3.0.

claims.statement: The reviewed subject is mc release RELEASE.2025-08-13T08-35-41Z at commit 7394ce0, the latest release tag.
claims.qualification: The inherited page recorded the version as unknown; groundwork pinned it to the latest release tag.

claims.statement: The full-bucket smoke run records the mc version as caller-supplied metadata, not a value the receipt independently measured.
claims.qualification: run.meta records tool_version_source=caller-supplied, so the release and commit identity of the ran image digest is not receipt-proven.

claims.statement: Upstream publishes an official multi-arch mc image for amd64, arm64, and ppc64le, alongside prebuilt dl.min.io binaries.
claims.qualification: Only the arm64 image was exercised; the other architectures and the dl.min.io binaries were not each run.

claims.statement: The full-bucket smoke listing ran on the native arm64 mc image with no emulation.
claims.qualification: run.meta records image_arch=arm64 and host_arch=arm64 for the run.

claims.statement: mc's testability is trivial: the official upstream image ran the listing modes anonymously with no patch, fork, or self-authored Dockerfile.
claims.qualification: The 'Trivial' testability judgment is supported by the native arm64 official image running to a verifier PASS; the other architectures and the dl.min.io binaries were not each exercised.

claims.statement: mc ls --recursive lists the full AWS S3 bucket completely, with 148,917 keys and no missing, extra, or duplicate keys.
claims.qualification: Exercised anonymously against AWS S3 only; the MinIO-server half of the inherited MinIO-and-generic-S3 claim is a separate unverified claim.

claims.statement: mc ls --recursive --json emits JSONL whose exact size, etag, mtime, and storageClass all match the manifest.
claims.qualification: The full-bucket --json run matched all four fields across 148,917 keys; --json is the only faithful output contract.

claims.statement: All ten anonymous smoke runs across the seven exercised listing modes PASS the study verifier for the fields each mode exposes.
claims.qualification: The complete set of ten receipts — recursive and delimiter ls at four scopes, --versions, and mc find, in text and JSON — each verified dups=0 missing=0 extra=0 fields=0 for the fields the mode exposes.

claims.statement: mc find and mc find --json list all STANDARD objects on the hourly prefix (2,549 keys) with no missing, extra, or duplicate keys.
claims.qualification: The PASS is scoped to STANDARD objects because the smoke bucket is entirely STANDARD.

claims.statement: mc find is a second listing surface that drives the same serial List() path as ls --recursive.
claims.qualification: find is a distinct traversal command but shares the underlying serial listing path.

claims.statement: mc find unconditionally skips GLACIER objects, so it is not a faithful full lister on buckets with archived objects.
claims.qualification: The all-STANDARD smoke bucket cannot exercise this completeness hole, so the find PASS receipts cannot expose it.

claims.statement: mc find emits no ETag even under --json.
claims.qualification: find's output contract omits ETag entirely; normalize.sh marks etag as '-' for both find modes.

claims.statement: mc lists through a single goroutine that issues one ListObjectsV2 request at a time, advancing by continuation-token, with no concurrent LIST calls.
claims.qualification: The debug trace shows serial pages each carrying the prior continuation-token and no concurrency; an [OBS] probe is not a run receipt, so packet-level proof is deferred to the replay phase.

claims.statement: Whether AWS or a MinIO server serves each LIST request internally in parallel is neither confirmed nor refuted by the available client-side evidence.
claims.qualification: The inherited 'server-internal parallelism' framing is not adopted as established fact; client evidence only shows no client-side fan-out. Rigorous proof is deferred to the replay-server phase.

evidence.reason: Client-side source and trace cannot settle how a server serves each request internally; deferred to the replay-server phase.

claims.statement: mc does no client-side keyspace division or fan-out; recursion is server-side via the absence of a delimiter.
claims.qualification: No client-side prefix split or key-range sharding flag exists.

claims.statement: mc hard-wires MaxKeys=-1 so it never sends max-keys, and no flag or env can change the server default page size of at most 1000.
claims.qualification: The SDK sets max-keys only when maxkeys is greater than zero, so mc's -1 means the server default page size is always taken.

claims.statement: mc's listing concurrency is one and no fan-out flag exists in mc ls or mc find.
claims.qualification: The single-goroutine paginator exposes neither page size nor concurrency as a tunable in mc ls's flag block or mc find's traversal, so there is nothing on the listing path for a benchmark to sweep. The absence rests on the recorded live --help inspection of both the ls and find surfaces and the pinned-source flag blocks, preserved in the research record; no help-surface receipt was committed.

claims.statement: mc was not exercised against a MinIO-server endpoint, so no AWS-vs-MinIO-server listing comparison exists.
claims.qualification: No MinIO-server endpoint is registered in the study; standing one up is a benchmark-phase infrastructure decision.

evidence.reason: The study registers only AWS buckets; no MinIO-server endpoint was available to exercise the both-endpoints comparison.

claims.statement: Whether mc has any MinIO-server-specific listing advantage that fails to generalize to AWS S3 is untested.
claims.qualification: This inherited performance question is not exercisable against the AWS-only smoke bucket.

evidence.reason: No MinIO server was run, so a MinIO-specific advantage could be neither reproduced nor refuted.

claims.statement: mc is inherited as designed to work well against MinIO servers specifically.
claims.qualification: This is an inherited design-intent assertion, distinct from the untested MinIO-specific performance advantage, and it was not exercisable against the AWS-only smoke bucket.

evidence.reason: The design-intent claim was not exercised; no MinIO-server endpoint was registered in the study.

claims.statement: mc is a mature project with a long release history of 317 release tags.
claims.qualification: Maturity is corroborated by the release-tag history.

claims.statement: The minio/mc repository is archived (read-only) on GitHub as of the 2026-07-17 API snapshot.
claims.qualification: archived:true with last push 2025-11-20 contradicts the inherited 'actively maintained' claim.

claims.statement: The pinned mc release is inferred to be effectively terminal.
claims.qualification: An archived repository can be unarchived, so this is a maintenance-posture inference, not a permanence proof.

claims.statement: mc has no Parquet or columnar listing output in mc ls or mc find.
claims.qualification: Neither listing surface exposes a Parquet or columnar output flag: mc ls's flag block offers only the text/JSON contract, and mc find's emit path prints only text or JSON. The absence rests on the recorded live --help inspection of both the ls and find surfaces and the pinned-source flag blocks, preserved in the research record; no help-surface receipt was committed.

claims.statement: mc exposes no user-facing resume flag, so an interrupted listing restarts from the beginning.
claims.qualification: mc ls's flag block exposes no resume or checkpoint flag, and the continuation-token lives entirely inside the SDK loop and is never surfaced. The absence rests on the recorded live --help inspection of both the ls and find surfaces and the pinned-source flag blocks, preserved in the research record; no help-surface receipt was committed.

claims.statement: mc's behavior on an interrupted or resumed listing was not exercised.
claims.qualification: No receipt exercised an interrupt or resume.

evidence.reason: No interrupt or resume scenario was run during groundwork.

claims.statement: No throughput figures were inherited for mc and groundwork adds none.
claims.qualification: Recorded smoke durations are non-comparative facts about individual runs, per methodology.

claims.statement: Every meaningful LIST decision lives in the minio-go v7.0.90 SDK; mc contributes the CLI, alias and credential model, and output formatting.
claims.qualification: V1/V2 selection, delimiter, pagination, retry, encoding, and the truncated-token guard are all in the SDK, so auditing mc's listing means auditing minio-go.

claims.statement: mc does not read AWS_ACCESS_KEY_ID or AWS_SECRET_ACCESS_KEY for signing; credentials come only from its alias, MC_HOST_*, or STS chain.
claims.qualification: This is a genuine capability distinction from aws-cli and rclone: it changes what anonymous access means for mc.

claims.statement: Anonymous access is an alias with empty keys, which minio-go resolves to SignatureAnonymous and then issues unsigned requests.
claims.qualification: The debug trace shows no Authorization header on any request, consistent with the empty-credential alias mechanism.

claims.statement: mc's default text output humanizes sizes and prints no ETag, so exact size and ETag are available only via --json.
claims.qualification: The text-mode smoke run exposed neither exact size nor ETag, consistent with the humanized, ETag-free text format.

claims.statement: Delimiter-listing folders are synthetic CommonPrefixes fabricated client-side with time.Now() timestamps and zero size, not objects.
claims.qualification: normalize.sh marks their mtime and size as '-'.

claims.statement: The text-mode key parse in the study adapter is best-effort, so *-json modes are authoritative for keys.
claims.qualification: A key beginning with a storage-class token and a space is indistinguishable from the SC column; whitespace-free NOAA keys parse exactly and general weird-key fidelity is deferred (EDGE_BUCKET=none).

claims.statement: mc ls --versions ran on the hourly prefix and returned each object once with the same key, size, etag, and mtime as the list-type=2 recursive listing of that prefix.
claims.qualification: On this unversioned bucket the versions listing produced the same 2,549-object set as the list-type=2 recursive listing of the same prefix.

claims.statement: Genuine multi-version collapse and delete-marker behavior were not exercised.
claims.qualification: Every object on the smoke bucket has one null version, so the versions mode never exercised true multi-version rows; deferred to a versioned edge fixture.

evidence.reason: The smoke bucket is unversioned, so multi-version fidelity could not be exercised.

claims.statement: minio-go bails on a truncated response with an empty continuation token rather than looping, a guard shared by every minio-go-based tool.
claims.qualification: The reachable guard raises a NotImplemented 'Truncated response should have continuation token set' error, so this is not an mc-specific or s3ls-rs-exclusive guard.

claims.statement: mc streams objects one at a time through channels and does not accumulate the full key set in memory for a plain listing.
claims.qualification: mc's contentCh is cap-0 and minio-go's objectStatCh is cap-1, so listing memory is bounded by design at smoke scale.

claims.statement: The full-bucket smoke run sampled a 35.4 MB peak RSS and a 16.1 MB cgroup peak.
claims.qualification: This is a single-run high-water mark, not a scaling result or benchmark.

claims.statement: mc's streaming memory footprint at millions of keys, and under --versions, is unconfirmed.
claims.qualification: The small groundwork runs cannot establish O(1) memory at scale; no object-count scaling campaign has been run.

evidence.reason: No object-count scaling campaign has been run.

claims.statement: minio-go retries each HTTP request up to ten attempts total with a 200ms exponential-jitter base, then streams the error and aborts the listing.
claims.qualification: MaxRetry is 10 and DefaultRetryUnit is 200ms; a page error surviving all attempts stops the listing.

claims.statement: mc's retry and backoff behavior under 503 throttling was not exercised.
claims.qualification: The SDK's 10x/200ms policy is untestable politely at smoke scale; no throttling or fault-injection run was performed.

evidence.reason: No 503 throttling or fault-injection run was performed at smoke scale.

claims.statement: The relative CPU and output cost of text versus --json formatting at scale was not measured.
claims.qualification: Smoke is not a comparative benchmark, so the per-format cost is a benchmark-phase question.

evidence.reason: No benchmark measured the per-output-format formatting cost.

claims.statement: The full-bucket --json smoke run's container wall-clock was 91.8 seconds.
claims.qualification: This is a non-comparative fact about one cross-internet run, not a benchmark result.

claims.statement: mc's serial full-bucket throughput ceiling has not been measured under benchmark conditions.
claims.qualification: With page size fixed at the server default and strict serial pagination, mc's full-bucket time is modelled as ceil(N/1000) x (RTT + page-parse); at 148,917 keys this was ~76-92s cross-internet, and in-region it is predicted to be RTT-bound and much faster though still serial. The serial wall-clock and per-page CPU are the benchmark-phase quantities to measure.

evidence.reason: No benchmark measured mc's serial throughput ceiling.

claims.statement: The --rewind, --incomplete, and --zip modes were recorded but not smoked.
claims.qualification: The bucket is unversioned so --rewind has nothing to resolve, it has no in-progress multipart uploads for --incomplete, and --zip is MinIO-only and N/A on AWS S3.

evidence.reason: --rewind has nothing to resolve on the unversioned bucket, --incomplete finds no in-progress multipart uploads on it, and --zip is unavailable because the x-minio-extract header is MinIO-only and N/A on AWS S3.
